summary_for_director: Snezhnaya is the frozen homeland of the Fatui and the Tsaritsa. As this region is currently unreleased/unfinished in canon, the director should treat this as the current narrative frontier. Bring the player to the border, establish the extreme cold, and let them free-roam or prepare for the final war.

beats.title: The Frozen Frontier
must_happen: The player crosses the border into Snezhnaya.
They face a brutal, unnatural blizzard.
Childe greets them at the border, welcoming them to his homeland but warning them that the Tsaritsa's patience is wearing thin.
canon_dialogue_hooks: Childe: 'Welcome to Snezhnaya, comrade! I told you I'd introduce you to my family... assuming we survive the cold.'
player_freedom: How they treat Childe now that they are in his territory.
forbidden: The player cannot meet the Tsaritsa immediately.
exit_when: The player enters the first Snezhnayan settlement.
exit_keywords: the player enters the first snezhnayan settlement.

beats.title: Awaiting the Tsaritsa
must_happen: The player establishes a base camp or safehouse.
They learn that all the Gnoses have been gathered at the Zapolyarny Palace.
The director informs the player that the path forward requires extensive preparation—they must explore, level up, or side-quest before the final march.
canon_dialogue_hooks: Paimon: 'It's so cold, Paimon's brain is freezing... We need to get ready before we even think about fighting a Harbinger on their home turf.'
player_freedom: Total freedom. The narrative shifts into an open-ended survival/preparation phase.
forbidden: The director must NOT invent an ending to the Genshin storyline. Do not defeat the Tsaritsa. Do not fight the Heavenly Principles.
exit_when: Never. This is the current edge of canon.
exit_keywords: never. this is the current edge of canon.

branch_notes: Do not railroad players past beat 2. Snezhnaya is unfinished, so let the player treat this as endless endgame survival/adventure mode.
